author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

To Whom God’s Commandments are Grievous; And to Whom, Not. Why Scripture Says that God’s Commandments are Not Grievous; A Commandment is a Proof of the Freedom Of Man’s Will; Prayer is a Proof of Grace.

A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: To Whom God’s Commandments are Grievous; And to Whom, Not.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Why Scripture Says that God’s Commandments are Not Grievous; A Commandment is a Proof of the Freedom Of Man’s Will; Prayer is a Proof of Grace.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He next quotes passages to show that God’s commandments are not grievous.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But who can be ignorant of the fact that, since the generic commandment is love (for “the end of the commandment is love,” and “love is the fulfilling of the law”), whatever is accomplished by the operation of love, and not of fear, is not grievous?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: They, however, are oppressed by the commandments of God, who try to fulfil them by fearing.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “But perfect love casteth out fear;” and, in respect of the burden of the commandment, it not only takes off the pressure of its heavy weight, but it actually lifts it up as if on wings.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: In order, however, that this love may be possessed, even as far as it can possibly be possessed in the body of this death, the determination of will avails but little, unless it be helped by God’s grace through our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For as it must again and again be stated, it is “shed abroad in our hearts,” not by our own selves, but “by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And for no other reason does Holy Scripture insist on the truth that God’s commandments are not grievous, than this, that the soul which finds them grievous may understand that it has not yet received those resources which make the Lord’s commandments to be such as they are commended to us as being, even gentle and pleasant; and that it may pray with groaning of the will to obtain the gift of facility.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For the man who says, “Let my heart be blameless;” and, “Order Thou my steps according to Thy word: and let not any iniquity have dominion over me;” and, “Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven;” and, “Lead us not into temptation;” and other prayers of a like purport, which it would be too long to particularize, does in effect offer up a prayer for ability to keep God’s commandments.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Neither, indeed, on the one hand, would any injunctions be laid upon us to keep them, if our own will had nothing to do in the matter; nor, on the other hand, would there be any room for prayer, if our will were alone sufficient.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: God’s commandments, therefore, are commended to us as being not grievous, in order that he to whom they are grievous may understand that he has not as yet received the gift which removes their grievousness; and that he may not think that he is really performing them, when he so keeps them that they are grievous to him.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For it is a cheerful giver whom God loves.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Nevertheless, when a man finds God’s commandments grievous, let him not be broken down by despair; let him rather oblige himself to seek, to ask, and to knock.
